memoryArtifacts.description: Origin Displayed: Irish - Origins Available: French, Irish. Spelling variations of this family name include: Roche, Roach, Roache, LaRoche, LaRoach, DeLaRoach, Roack, Roch, Roiche, St.Roche, Rocheland, Rochellan and many more. First found in county Limerick where they were granted lands by Strongbow whom they accompanied into Ireland during the Anglo-Norman invasion of 1172. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: William Roach settled in Virginia in 1707; Edmund, Frederick, James, John, Margaret, Mary, Michael, Patrick, Richard, Thomas and William Roach all arrived in Philadelphia between 1840 and 1870. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009). Motto Translated: My God is my rock. Recorded as de Roche, Deroche, Le Roche, Roach, Roache, Roches, Roches and possibly others, this is a surname of French origins well recorded in both England and Ireland. It is residential, and described somebody who lived by a rocky crag or who came from "Les Roches," a place in Normandy. From this origin it became widespread in England after the Norman invasion of England in 1066, spread to Ireland after 1170 where it became widespread in the province. The place name Rochestown occurs in the counties of Wexford, Cork and Kilkenny, and the family were particularly powerful family in Fermoy, County Cork, where a large tract of land is still known as "Roches county". Early recordings of the name in England include Lucas de Roches of Hampshire in 1249, and Ralph de la Roche in the Pipe Rolls of Cornwall in 1195. William Roache is recorded as a small landowner in the new world of Barbados, in the West Indies, circa 1678 - 1680. Father Philip Roche of Wexford died for his prominent part in the 1798 Rebellion in Ireland, as did one Edward Roche. Sir Boyle Roche (1743 - 1807) was famous for his wit and "bulls". The first recorded spelling of the family name is shown to be that of John de Roches. This was dated 1086, in the Domesday Book of Bedfordshire, during the reign of King William, known as "The Conqueror", 1066 - 1087. Surnames became necessary when governments introduced personal taxation. In England this was sometimes known as Poll Tax. Throughout the centuries, surnames in every country have continued to "develop" often leading to astonishing variants of the original spelling.  Copyright: Name Origin Research www.surnamedb.com 1980 - 2014.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John De La Roche於1357年出生在英國維爾爾布洛哈姆時，他的父親Sir John De La Roche77歲，母親Agnes Berwick45歲。他和Willelma De La Mare至少有2個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1400年9月30日在英國享年43歲，埋葬在英國維爾爾布洛哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: La
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Roche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Aidan, Declan, Eamonn, Fergus, Fionnuala, Kevin Patrick, Liam.
Irish (Munster) and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Roach  . Compare   Roch  .

French: topographic name for someone who lived by a rocky outcrop or crag, from Old French roche ‘rock’. In North America, this surname is also a shortened form of the variant Laroche 2. Compare Deroche .

variants.name: Laroche
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Fernand, Normand, Pierre, Jacques, Laurent, Adrien, Cecile, Germain, Marcel.
French:
habitational name from any of numerous places in various parts of France named Laroche or La Roche, from Old French roche ‘rock’, with feminine definite article la.

variants.name: Larocque
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Fernand, Henri, Marcel, Normand, Valmore, Yves, Achille, Amie, Donat, Emile.

French: habitational name from La Ro(c)que, the name of several places in various parts of France, from a regional variant of roche ‘rock’ (see Roche , compare Laroche ), with feminine definite article la. Compare Laroque .

Altered form of southern French Roquebrune or of its variant Rocquebrune: habitational name from Ro(c)quebrune, the name of several places in the southern part of France. The surname Roquebrune is rare in France, while the variant Rocquebrune is extinct there. Compare Larock , Lerocque , Rockburn , and Rocque 1.

variants.name: Rochet
meanings: French: diminutive of   Roche  , a topographic name meaning ‘rock’, or a habitational name from (Le) Rochet, the name of several places in various parts of France.
Catalan: of French origin (see above). This surname is also found in the Dominican Republic and Puerto Rico.

variants.name: Rochat
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Monique, Philippe, Pierre.
French: topographic name from a diminutive of Old French roche ‘rock’ (see Roche 2), or a habitational name from Le Rochat, the name of several places in the eastern part of France.

variants.name: Roach
meanings: English:

of Norman origin, from Old French and Middle English roche ‘rock, cliff, promontory’, sometimes a translation of Stone . The surname may have been topographic, denoting someone who lived on or by a prominent rock or rocky place, or a habitational name for someone who lived or came from a place so named, such as one of those called Roque or Roche(s) in Normandy and neighboring regions, or Roche in Cornwall, Roach Farm in Clyst Hydon (Devon), or Roch in Pembrokeshire (see Roch ).

in Lancashire perhaps referring to the River Roch, which runs through Rochdale; the river name is probably a back-formation from Recedham, an early name for Rochdale recorded in 1086, derived from Old English reced ‘building, house, hall’ + hām ‘village, homestead’. Alternatively, the Lancashire name could be a shortened form of the now rare or extinct surname Rochdale alias Rachedale, Rachdall, a habitational name from Rochdale (Lancashire). From the 18th century onward the name is difficult to distinguish from Roch , borne by Irish migrants to South Lancashire, especially Liverpool.

variants.name: Roquet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alain, Camille.
French: topographic name from a diminutive of roche ‘rock’ (see Roche and Roque ), or a habitational name from (Le) Roquet, the name of several places in various parts of France.

variants.name: Rochez
meanings: Walloon and French: variant of   Roche   2 or   Rocher  .

nameMeanings.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John De La Roche was born in 1357, in Bromham, Wiltshire, England, his father, Sir John De La Roche, was 77 and his mother, Agnes Berwick, was 45. He had at least 2 sons and 2 daughters with Willelma De La Mare. He died on 30 September 1400, in England, at the age of 43, and was buried in Bromham, Wiltshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John De La Roche est né en 1357, à Bromham, Wiltshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John De La Roche, avait 77 ans et sa mère, Agnes Berwick, avait 45 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles avec Willelma De La Mare. Il est décédé le 30 septembre 1400, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterré à Bromham, Wiltshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John De La Roche 1357 in Bromham, Wiltshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John De La Roche 77 und seine Mutter Agnes Berwick 45. Er hatte mit Willelma De La Mare mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 30. September 1400 im Alter von 43 Jahren in England und wurde in Bromham, Wiltshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John De La Roche è nato il nel 1357 nel Bromham, Wiltshire, England, il suo padre, Sir John De La Roche, aveva 77 anni e il suo madre, Agnes Berwick, aveva 45 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie con Willelma De La Mare. Morì il 30 settembre 1400, nel England, all'età di 43 anni, e fu sepolto a Bromham, Wiltshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John De La Rocheが1357年にイギリスウィルトシュアイアブロムハムで生まれた時，父Sir John De La Rocheは77歳，母Agnes Berwickは45歳であった。彼はWillelma De La Mareとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1400年9月30日にイギリスで43歳で死去し，イギリスウィルトシュアイアブロムハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John De La Roche(이)가 1357년 영국 윌츠히르 브롬함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John De La Roche(은)는 77세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Berwick(은)는 45세였다. 그는 Willelma De La Mare(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1400년 9월 30일 영국에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 윌츠히르 브롬함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John De La Roche nasceu em 1357, em Bromham, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John De La Roche, tinha 77 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Berwick, tinha 45 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas com Willelma De La Mare. Ele faleceu em 30 de setembro de 1400, em Inglaterra, com 43 anos, e foi sepultado em Bromham, Wiltshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John De La Roche родился в 1357 г. в местности бромхам, уилцхир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John De La Roche было 77 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Berwick, 45. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери от Willelma De La Mare. Он умер 30 сентября 1400 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 43 лет и был похоронен в местности бромхам, уилцхир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John De La Roche nació en 1357, en Bromham, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John De La Roche, tenía 77 años y su mamá, Agnes Berwick, tenía 45 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas con Willelma De La Mare. Murió el 30 de septiembre de 1400, en Inglaterra, a la edad de 43 años, y fue sepultado en Bromham, Wiltshire, Inglaterra.
